name: Citrus Marinade & Salsa (for Chicken or Fish)
recipeIngredient: 2 tablespoons Olive oil or cooking oil
1 medium Tomato, peeled, seeded and Chopped
recipeInstructions:
Servings: 1
For marinade, in a 1-cup measure combine lemon juice, sherry, oil, oregano, and garlic salt. Set aside 2 tablespoons. Pour remaining into a plastic bag; add chicken or fish. Close bag; chill for 1 hour, turning once.
For salsa, combine the 2 tablespoons marinade, tomato, green onion, olives, parsley, and almonds. Cover; chill until serving time.
Drain chicken or fish, reserving marinade. Grill until chicken is tender and fish is flaky, brushing often with the marinade. Serve with chilled salsa and avocado slices, if desired.
Makes 4 servings.
Nutrition facts per tablespoon salsa: 13 calories, 0 total fat (0 saturated fat), 0 mg cholesterol, 13 mg sodium, 1 gram carbohydrates, 0 grams fiber, 0 grams protein. Daily value: 1% Vitamin A, 5% Vitamin C, 0% Calcium, 0% Iron.
SOURCE: BETTER HOMES AND GARDENS, July 1993
Shared by Cate Vanicek
